original_hypothesis.title: setUSDThresholds Allows Zero or Extreme Values Breaking Price Governance
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setUSDThresholds has no bounds validation on _approve or _disapprove values. Admin can set DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD=0, which makes `disapprovalsCount < 0` always false for uint8 (impossible condition), permanently blocking ALL price update executions in _execUSDPriceUpdate. Conversely, setting APPROVE_THRESHOLD=0 allows unintended behavior since no approval count can be less than 0 for uint8.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The _execUSDPriceUpdate condition requires `disapprovalsCount < DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD`. With DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD=0, this is `uint8 < 0` which is always false, making it impossible to execute any price update regardless of approval count. No recovery path exists within the contract since setUSDThresholds requires no active proposal.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No minimum/maximum bounds on `_approve` or `_disapprove` parameters; _execUSDPriceUpdate requires `disapprovalsCount < DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD` which is impossible when threshold=0

preconditions: Admin sets DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD to 0 (or other extreme value)
No active proposal at time of threshold change

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No bounds checking on uint8 threshold values
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent until admin corrects the threshold

operational_questions: What values have been historically set for thresholds?
Is there a multisig process protecting the admin key from misconfiguration?
Can the admin fix the issue by resetting thresholds (yes, if no active proposal)?
validation_requirements: Set DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD=0, propose and approve price update, verify execution is impossible

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All subsequent price proposals cannot execute: `disapprovalsCount < 0` is always false
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: USD prices become permanently stale, distorting all weighted value calculations
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Admin must realize the error and reset threshold (requires no active proposal)

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Recoverable if admin is aware — set threshold back to valid value
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All USD price updates, weighted value calculations

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: None — simple function call with wrong parameter

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Price proposals never executing despite sufficient approvals

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
